view.rsu.hint.intro: Κάθε γιλέκο: FMV στο γιλέκο = συνηθισμένο εισόδημα (W-2 κουτί 1), συμπληρωματικό W/H 22% (37% πάνω από $1 εκατομμύριο YTD). Προεπιλογή: sell-to-cover στο γιλέκο, κρατάτε τα υπόλοιπα. Βάση = FMV στο γιλέκο. Μεταγενέστερη πώληση: κέρδος ή ζημιά από γιλέκο FMV. Το συμπληρωματικό W/H 22% είναι συχνά ανεπαρκές σε υψηλά εισοδήματα — πραγματοποιήστε εκτιμώμενες πληρωμές για να αποφύγετε την έκπληξη του Απριλίου.
view.rsu.note: Εάν το ομοσπονδιακό έλλειμμα > 1.000 $, μπορεί να οφείλετε εκτιμώμενο φόρο ή πρόστιμο για το τέταρτο τρίμηνο. Προσαρμόστε το W-4 («πρόσθετη παρακράτηση») ή πληρώστε 1040-ES Q4.
view.s1014.h1.title: // § 1014 STEP-UP AT DEATH
view.s1014.hint.intro: Heir's basis = FMV at decedent's death (or alternate valuation 6 months later). Wipes built-in gain accrued during decedent's lifetime. § 1014(c) IRD exception: NO step-up for traditional IRA / 401(k) / annuity / NQ deferred comp. Joint property: only decedent's half steps up. Community property (AZ, CA, ID, LA, NV, NM, TX, WA, WI + AK opt-in): BOTH halves step up. Most powerful estate-planning rule.
view.s1014.strat.aging_parent_dollar: 'Aging parent dollar': gift cash to elderly parent + they buy appreciated asset → step-up at parent's death → reinherit
view.s1014.strat.community_lookback: Community property: BOTH halves step up — vs JTWROS (only decedent's)
view.s1014.strat.hold: Hold appreciated assets in taxable estate until death (vs. selling pre-death)
view.s1014.strat.idgt: IDGT: grantor trust during life, step-up if power swap exercised at death
view.s1014.strat.qtip: QTIP marital deduction property step-up at SECOND spouse's death
view.s1014.strat.roth_pre_death: Roth conversion before death = step-up wasted (already tax-free); for IRD assets, Roth convert PRE-death
view.s1014.strat.section_2036: § 2036 retained life estate: includable in estate → full step-up
view.s1014.strat.section_2038: § 2038 retained powers: keep assets in estate intentionally for step-up
view.s1014.strat.upstream: 'Upstream gifting' to elderly parents: they die, full step-up, you reinherit
view.s1014.warning.ird: § 1014(c) IRD exception: no basis step-up. Heir owes ordinary income tax on distributions. § 691(c) deduction available for estate tax attributable to IRD.
view.s1015.db.appreciation: If FMV at gift ≥ donor's basis: STANDARD carryover (single basis = donor's basis)
view.s1015.db.depreciation: If FMV at gift < donor's basis: DUAL BASIS applies
view.s1015.db.example: Example: donor's basis $100K, FMV at gift $80K; sale at $90K = NO GAIN / NO LOSS
view.s1015.db.gain_basis: FOR GAIN: use donor's basis (no recognition of pre-gift loss)
view.s1015.db.loss_basis: FOR LOSS: use FMV at gift (no recognition of pre-gift depreciation)
view.s1015.db.zone: 'No-gain / no-loss zone': sale price between FMV at gift and donor's basis
view.s1015.gtb.add_to_basis: Adjusted basis = donor's basis + gift tax adjustment
view.s1015.gtb.appreciation: Net appreciation = FMV at gift − donor's basis
view.s1015.gtb.cannot_exceed_fmv: Adjusted basis cannot EXCEED FMV at gift (no further step-up)
view.s1015.gtb.example: Example: donor's basis $50K, FMV $200K, annual excl $19K, gift tax $30K. Adjustment = $30K × ($150K / $181K) = $24,862
view.s1015.gtb.formula: Adjustment = gift tax × (net appreciation / net gift)
view.s1015.gtb.net_gift: Net gift = FMV at gift − annual exclusion used ($19K 2025)
view.s1015.gtb.who_paid: Applied when DONOR PAID gift tax (not donee)
view.s1015.h1.title: // § 1015 GIFT BASIS
view.s1015.h2.dual_basis: Dual basis rule (loss / gain different basis)
view.s1015.hint.intro: CARRYOVER basis: donee takes donor's adjusted basis. Holding period TACKS from donor's. Dual basis rule: if FMV at gift < donor's basis: GAIN uses donor's basis; LOSS uses FMV at gift. 'NO GAIN / NO LOSS' zone: between FMV at gift and donor's basis. § 1015(d)(6) gift tax adjustment: add portion of gift tax attributable to net appreciation. Spousal gift: § 1041 carryover basis + no gift tax (with elections). Compared to § 1014: death gives FULL STEP-UP to FMV (gift loses this).
view.s1015.no_gain_loss_note: Sale falls in 'no-gain / no-loss zone' between FMV at gift and donor's basis. No gain or loss recognized — donor's pre-gift unrealized loss is PERMANENTLY LOST. Plan accordingly: hold or sell at lower price for tax loss benefit.
view.s1015.plan.529: § 529 plans: special 5-yr forward gift treatment (5× annual exclusion)
view.s1015.plan.annual_exclusion: Use annual exclusion ($19K 2025) for gift tax-free transfers
view.s1015.plan.death_better_appreciated: DEATH is BETTER for highly appreciated assets (full step-up vs carryover)
view.s1015.plan.gift_better_depreciated: GIFT is BETTER for depreciating assets (no loss waste at carryover basis)
view.s1015.plan.gst: GST tax: separate exemption for generation-skipping transfers
view.s1015.plan.med_education: Direct medical / educational payments: NOT counted (no $19K limit)
view.s1015.plan.unified_credit: 2026 sunset: unified credit may drop ~$7M unless extended
view.s102.dub.consideration: Past or implied consideration defeats gift status
view.s102.dub.constraining: NOT constraining force / moral duty / business expectation
view.s102.dub.context: Context: family vs business; surprise vs anticipated; quid-pro-quo
view.s102.dub.crowdfunding: Crowdfunding / GoFundMe: typically gift if non-business + no return expected
view.s102.dub.intent: Donor's intent CONTROLS (objective evidence of motivation)
view.s102.dub.test: "Detached and disinterested generosity" — out of love, affection, respect, charity
view.s102.dub.tips: Tips: NOT gifts (Olk v. United States) — even if voluntary
view.s102.dub.windfall: Windfall + lottery: NOT gift (winner has earned via game)
view.s102.h1.title: // § 102 GIFT / INHERITANCE EXCLUSION
view.s102.hint.intro: § 102(a) exclusion. § 102(c) employer→employee presumption of compensation. Duberstein detached + disinterested generosity. § 1015 carryover / § 1014 step-up.
view.s102.s1014.alt: § 2032 alternate valuation date: 6 months after death (if elected at estate level)
view.s102.s1014.beneficiary_step_up: Inherited Roth IRA: still tax-free + 10-year rule (post-SECURE)
view.s102.s1014.community_property: Community property double step-up at first death (§ 1014(b)(6))
view.s102.s1014.general: Inherited property: basis = FMV at decedent's DATE OF DEATH
view.s102.s1014.s1014_e: § 1014(e) — gift within 1 year of death + back to donor: NO step-up
view.s102.s1014.s2056_marital: § 2056 marital deduction unlimited — no estate tax + step-up preserved
view.s102.s1014.s691_c: § 691(c) estate tax deduction on IRD — offsets income tax on IRD
view.s102.s1014.unrealized_gain_extingui: Unrealized gain at death: ELIMINATED — major estate planning benefit
view.s102.s1015.bargain_sale: Bargain sale: basis allocation between sale portion + gift portion
view.s102.s1015.dual_basis: § 1015(a) dual basis: for LOSS purposes = LESSER of donor basis OR FMV at gift
view.s102.s1015.f709: Form 709 — gift tax return (annual exclusion + lifetime exemption)
view.s102.s1015.formula: Adjustment = gift tax × (net appreciation / FMV at gift)
view.s102.s1015.gift_tax_adj: § 1015(d) basis adjustment: + portion of gift tax paid attributable to net appreciation
view.s102.s1015.holding_tacks: Holding period TACKS — donee holds since donor's acquisition
view.s102.s1015.s2701_2704: § 2701-2704 — special valuation rules for family transfers (estate planning)
view.s102.s102c.applies: Transfer from EMPLOYER to EMPLOYEE (current or former) presumed comp
view.s102.s102c.bona_fide_owner: Owner-employee from personal funds: may rebut presumption (facts & circumstances)
view.s102.s102c.business_purpose: Business purpose evidence: pay-for-performance vs gift on personal occasion
view.s102.s102c.death_benefit: Employer death benefit to family: § 102(c) presumed but rebuttal possible (§ 101(b))
view.s102.s102c.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents disguising compensation as gift
view.s102.s102c.s132_b: § 132(b) de minimis fringe benefits — separately excluded
view.s102.spec.bribery: Bribes: § 162(c) NOT deductible — NOT gift to recipient (illegal)
view.s102.spec.crowdfunding: Crowdfunding (GoFundMe etc.): facts & circumstances — typically gift if donative intent
view.s102.spec.kickback_disguised: "Kickbacks" disguised as gifts: § 162(c) — not gift, illegal payment
view.s102.spec.political: Political gifts to campaigns: tax-free to candidate (§ 527)
view.s102.spec.s74_b_charitable_exceptio: § 74(b) — Nobel Prize/similar: excluded if assigned to charity
view.s102.spec.scholarship: § 117 scholarship: tax-free for qualified educational expenses
view.s102.spec.support_payments: Support payments to family: typically gift (§ 102(a)) — not income
view.s102.spec.tips: Tips: § 61 ordinary income (NOT gift) — even when voluntary
view.s102.tbl.length_safety: Length of service / safety achievement only
view.s102.tbl.no_cash: NO cash, cash equivalent, gift card, stocks
view.s1031.h1.title: // § 1031 ΑΝΤΑΛΛΑΓΗ ΟΜΟΙΟΥ ΕΙΔΟΥΣ
view.s1031.hint.intro: Μόνο ακίνητα (μετά το TCJA 2018). Αυστηρές προθεσμίες: 45 ημέρες για την έγγραφη αναγνώριση της ιδιοκτησίας αντικατάστασης, 180 ημέρες για το κλείσιμο. Μπότα (μετρητά, ελάφρυνση χρέους) = φορολογητέα. Η βάση συνεχίζει. Θάνατος = βάση ενίσχυσης εξαλείφει το αναβαλλόμενο κέρδος. Απαιτείται QI — δεν μπορώ να αγγίξω τα έσοδα.
view.s1033.fail_note: Deferral failed — replacement outside window OR not 'same or similar' / 'like-kind'. Full gain recognized currently. Consider amended return + extended election if facts allow.
view.s1033.h1.title: // § 1033 INVOLUNTARY CONVERSION
view.s1033.hint.intro: Defer gain if proceeds REINVESTED in qualified replacement property within 2-yr window (3-yr real property condemnation, 4-yr federally declared disaster). 'Same or similar use' test (property & business owner-user); 'like-kind' test (investor-lessor § 1033(g)). Basis of replacement = cost MINUS deferred gain. Holding period TACKS. § 121 may also apply for principal residence. § 165 alternative: deductible loss. Form 1040 + statement.
view.s1033.qual.condemned_real: Condemned real property: like-kind real prop in trade / biz / investment qualifies
view.s1033.qual.controlled_corp: § 1033(a)(2)(A) replacement via 80%+ controlled corp acquisition allowed
view.s1033.qual.election_timely: Election: by filing return for year of gain (extended return + amended w/in 3 yrs)
view.s1033.qual.gain_only: Gain ONLY — loss not deferred under § 1033 (deductible under § 165)
view.s1033.qual.no_inventory: Inventory or stock in trade NOT eligible as replacement
view.s1033.qual.owner_user: Owner-user: 'same or similar use' — stricter test, functional equivalence
view.s1033.qual.related_party: § 1033(i) related party: limited to certain real property situations
view.s1033.s121.combine: Casualty of home: § 121 exclusion FIRST, then § 1033 deferral on remaining gain
view.s1033.s121.example: Example: $700K proceeds, $150K basis = $550K gain; § 121 excludes $500K → $50K § 1033 deferral on remainder
view.s1033.s121.exclusion: § 121: $250K / $500K MFJ exclusion on principal residence sale (2 of 5 yr ownership + use)
view.s1033.s121.partial: Partial use § 121: reduced exclusion + § 1033 on remainder
view.s1033.s121.window: § 121 has no replacement window (used once per 2 yrs); § 1033 deferral additional
view.s1035.boot.cash: Cash received = boot, gain to extent of gain
view.s1035.boot.loan: Loan extinguished by new carrier = boot (Estate of Halby; Greene)
view.s1035.boot.mec_carry: MEC contract: new contract IS ALSO MEC (no laundering)
view.s1035.boot.owner_assignee: Owner / assignee / insured must match (per Rev. Proc. 92-44)
view.s1035.boot.partial: Partial exchange + distribution within 180 days = taxable
view.s1035.h1.title: // § 1035 INSURANCE / ANNUITY EXCHANGE
view.s1035.hint.intro: Tax-free replacement of insurance / annuity / endowment / LTC contracts. One-way street: annuity → life insurance NOT allowed; life → annuity OK. Basis carries over. Loss NEVER recognized. Pension Protection Act 2006: partial exchanges allowed but must follow Rev. Proc. 2011-38: 180-day rule + no distributions for 180 days from either contract.
view.s1035.use.cheaper: Move VA to lower-cost VA (M&E + admin saves $30-100k over 20 yrs)
view.s1035.use.consolidate: Consolidate multiple policies for simpler estate admin
view.s1035.use.guaranteed: Move to better income / death benefit / GMxB rider
view.s1035.use.ltc: Move outdated LTC to modern hybrid LTC-life
view.s1035.use.no_lockout: Move past surrender-charge period to fresh investment options
view.s1035.use.term_to_whole: Convert term life to whole life
view.s1035.warning.disallowed: Exchange NOT allowed under § 1035 — transaction treated as TAXABLE SURRENDER of old contract + purchase of new. Entire gain recognized.
view.s1041.alm.child_support: Child support: NEVER deductible to payor / income to payee
view.s1041.alm.lump_sum: Lump-sum payments: scrutinized — § 71(c) recharacterizes if disguised property settlement
view.s1041.alm.modification: Modification of pre-2019 decree: keep old rules UNLESS new agreement specifies new
view.s1041.alm.post_tcja: Post-12/31/2018: alimony NEITHER deductible nor includible
view.s1041.alm.pre_2019: Pre-12/31/2018 divorces: alimony DEDUCTIBLE to payor; INCLUDIBLE to payee
view.s1041.alm.property_settlement: Property settlement (§ 1041): always tax-neutral (no income / deduction)
view.s1041.alm.retiree: Retiree planning: marital settlement around retirement assets via QDRO is critical
view.s1041.alm.state_law: State law: not all states followed TCJA — some allow state-level deduction
view.s1041.basis.carryover: Recipient takes CARRYOVER basis = transferor's adjusted basis
view.s1041.basis.gift_tax_inapplicable: NO gift tax adjustment (no gift tax paid since spousal transfer)
view.s1041.basis.later_sale: Later sale by recipient: gain / loss based on carryover basis
view.s1041.basis.liabilities: Liabilities assumed by recipient REDUCE basis (Crane rule)
view.s1041.basis.no_dual: NO dual basis (contrast § 1015 gift rule for depreciated property)
view.s1041.basis.nondeductible: No transferor loss recognition even if FMV < basis
view.s1041.h1.title: // § 1041 SPOUSE / DIVORCE
view.s1041.hint.intro: Transfers between spouses + transfers incident to divorce (within 1 yr OR pursuant to divorce decree) = NO GAIN OR LOSS. Recipient takes CARRYOVER BASIS from transferor. Holding period TACKS. Trust for spouse also qualifies. EXCEPTION: § 1041 does NOT apply if recipient is nonresident alien spouse (NRA carve-out). Post-TCJA 2017: alimony NO LONGER deductible / includible (for divorces post-12/31/2018). QDRO (Qualified Domestic Relations Order) for retirement assets — separate rules.
view.s1041.nra_note: NONRESIDENT ALIEN spouse: § 1041 does NOT apply. Transferor recognizes FULL gain/loss on transfer at FMV. Critical planning point — NRA spouse acquires basis = FMV but transferor pays tax now. Consider NRA election to be treated as US resident (§ 6013(g)) to access § 1041, but creates US-tax on worldwide income for NRA spouse.
view.s1041.scope.6_year: 6-year presumption: pursuant to decree if w/in 6 yrs
view.s1041.scope.assumption: Liabilities > basis (negative basis): § 1041 still applies (no recharacterization)
view.s1041.scope.beyond_6: Beyond 6 yrs: rebuttable presumption — must show divorce nexus
view.s1041.scope.incident: 'Incident to divorce': within 1 yr OR pursuant to decree
view.s1041.scope.installment: Installment note: § 1041 applies to original transfer; § 453B(g) for note received
view.s1041.scope.nra_exception: NONRESIDENT ALIEN spouse: § 1041 does NOT apply (gain recognized)
view.s1041.scope.trust: Transfer to trust for spouse's benefit: § 1041
view.s1041.spec.401k: 401(k) / qualified plans: QDRO required — TAXABLE if not properly transferred
view.s1041.spec.business_interests: Business interests: pre-divorce valuation often required + tax-free shifts to spouse
view.s1041.spec.deferred_comp: Deferred comp / pension splits: QDRO required to avoid acceleration; § 409A trap
view.s1041.spec.installment_note: Installment notes: § 453B(g) — recipient takes carryover basis + no gain on transfer
view.s1041.spec.iras: IRAs: transfer pursuant to divorce decree NOT taxable; QDRO not for IRA (use trustee-to-trustee)
view.s1041.spec.options: Stock options / RSU: vested options to ex-spouse via § 1041; tax due at exercise by recipient
view.s1041.spec.s_corp_stock: S-corp stock: § 1041 ok; recipient becomes shareholder for pass-through
view.s1042.h1.title: // § 1042 ESOP GAIN DEFERRAL
view.s1042.hint.intro: Sell qualified stock of closely-held domestic C-corp to ESOP that owns ≥ 30% post-sale. Reinvest proceeds in qualified replacement property (QRP) within 12 months (or 3 mo prior). Cap gain DEFERRED until QRP sold. Basis carries over. Estate planning hack: die holding QRP → step-up at death wipes out deferred gain (§ 1014). Massive succession + tax tool for retiring founders.
view.s1042.qrp.50_pct_rule: Issuer must derive < 25% passive income and have ≥ 50% assets active
view.s1042.qrp.diversify: Limited diversification options — managed-account QRP common
view.s1042.qrp.floating_rate: Common QRP: 'floating-rate notes' of operating cos (low risk + § 1042-qualified)
view.s1042.qrp.margin_loan: Can use margin loan against QRP to monetize without triggering gain
view.s1042.qrp.not_qualified: NOT: govt bonds, mutual funds, REITs, MLPs, S-corps, foreign corps
view.s1042.qrp.qualified: Stock OR bonds of US OPERATING companies
view.s1042.req.c_corp: Selling shareholder of domestic C-corp (not S-corp; S → § 1042 unavailable but S-ESOP has tax-free advantages)
view.s1042.req.esop_30pct: ESOP owns ≥ 30% of total outstanding stock immediately AFTER sale
view.s1042.req.esop_statement: ESOP files statement consenting to § 4978 excise on early disposition
view.s1042.req.no_family: Seller / family + 25% shareholders can't receive ESOP allocations for 10 yrs
view.s1042.req.notice: File Notice of Election (§ 1042(c)(3)(B)) with return
view.s1042.req.qrp_window: Acquire QRP within 12 months after sale (or 3 mo before, with Notice of Election)
view.s105.disc.benefits: Benefits test: same benefits available to all participants
view.s105.disc.cure: Cure: equalize benefits across HCE / non-HCE
view.s105.disc.eligibility: Eligibility test: 70%+ non-HCE OR 80%+ benefits non-HCE
view.s105.disc.formula: Excess = (HCE benefit − average non-HCE benefit) for that HCE
view.s105.disc.insurance_carrier_safe: Insured plans NOT subject to § 105(h) — bypass via insurance
view.s105.disc.s162m: Public companies: + § 162(m) $1M deduction limit on HCE comp
view.s105.disc.timing: Test applied on year-end basis (last day of year)
view.s105.disc.violations: Violations: HCE taxable on 'excess reimbursements' + Soc Sec
view.s105.disc_note: § 105(h) discrimination: excess reimbursements TAXABLE to HCE. Includes both regular + Social Security earnings. Cure: equalize benefits across HCE / non-HCE OR switch to INSURED plan (not subject to § 105(h)). Common issue in self-funded health plans of large employers.
view.s105.h1.title: // § 105 ACCIDENT + HEALTH
view.s105.hint.intro: § 105(a): Amounts under employer accident / health plan EXCLUDED from gross income. § 105(b): Medical care reimbursement — full exclusion. § 105(c): permanent loss of bodily function payments — excluded. § 105(h): SELF-INSURED medical plans — discriminatory amounts TAXABLE to highly compensated employees (HCE). § 162(l) SE health deduction: SE persons + 2% S-corp owners deduct ABOVE-THE-LINE. HRA / HSA / FSA use § 105 for tax-free reimbursement.
view.s105.l.aca_marketplace: Marketplace coverage premiums qualify (must self-fund or have employer not offer)
view.s105.l.limit: Limited to earned income from business (cannot create loss)
view.s105.l.no_em_plan: NOT eligible if employee + spouse employee plan available
view.s105.l.no_se: Deduction does NOT reduce SE tax (separate computation)
view.s105.l.s_corp_w2: S-corp owners: premiums must be ON W-2 (Box 1) to be deductible by shareholder
view.s105.l.spouse_dependents: Includes premiums for self, spouse, dependents, kids < 27 yrs
view.s105.l.who: Eligible: sole prop, partner, 2%+ S-corp owner, member of multimember LLC
view.s105.sub.a: § 105(a): amounts under employer plan — EXCLUDED from gross income (default rule)
view.s105.sub.b: § 105(b): medical care reimbursement up to actual expenses — full exclusion
view.s105.sub.c: § 105(c): permanent loss / disfigurement payments — excluded (regardless of wages)
view.s105.sub.d: § 105(d): wage / salary replacement (sick pay) — INCLUDED in income
view.s105.sub.h: § 105(h): self-insured plans discrimination — HCE taxable on excess benefits
view.s105.sub.s125: § 125 cafeteria plan + § 105: pre-tax salary reduction → reimbursement → no income
view.s105.types.cba: Cafeteria Plan § 125: choice between cash + tax-free benefits
view.s105.types.cobra: COBRA premium subsidy: § 6432 + ARPA temporary subsidies
view.s105.types.dcfsa: Dependent Care FSA: $5K limit, child care + elderly care
view.s105.types.fsa: FSA (Flexible Spending Account): salary reduction, use-it-or-lose-it
view.s105.types.hra: HRA (Health Reimbursement Arrangement): employer-funded, reimburses medical
view.s105.types.hsa: HSA (Health Savings Account): individual + employer combined, requires HDHP
view.s105.types.ichra: ICHRA (Individual Coverage HRA): premium reimbursement for individual plans
view.s105.types.qsehra: QSEHRA (Qualified Small Employer HRA): small biz ≤ 50 employees
view.s1058.borrow.s1058_c: § 1058(c) — substitute payments paid by borrower = deductible (subject to § 263(g))
view.s1058.borrow.s1233: § 1233 short sale rules apply to borrower
view.s1058.borrow.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale if borrower has appreciated position
view.s1058.borrow.s1259_d: § 1259(c) constructive sale exceptions: closed within 30 days + maintained
view.s1058.borrow.s263_g: § 263(g) capitalization of fees + substitute payments to carrying charges
view.s1058.borrow.s871m_paymt: § 871(m) dividend equivalent — borrower pays + withholds 30% on NRA payee
view.s1058.fail.basis_collateral: Collateral becomes lender's basis in cash received
view.s1058.fail.constructive_sale: § 1259 may apply to lender's position if borrower-disposal triggers
view.s1058.fail.fail_to_borrow: Fail-to-borrow buy-in: collateral converted to cash, securities buy-back required
view.s1058.fail.holding_resets: Holding period of returned securities starts new (Reg § 1.1058-2(b)(3))
view.s1058.fail.identical_returned: If non-identical securities returned: gain/loss on difference
view.s1058.fail.recognition: Recognition event — treated as SALE at FMV (gain to lender)
view.s1058.h1.title: // § 1058 SECURITIES LOAN NONRECOGNITION
view.s1058.hint.intro: Securities loan = nonrecognition if identical securities returned + substitute payments + at-will + economic ownership retained. Substitute payments ordinary not qualified.
view.s1058.req.at_will: Agreement terminable on DEMAND (at-will) by lender
view.s1058.req.collateral_typical: Typical: 102-105% of FMV cash collateral, daily mark-to-market
view.s1058.req.documentation: Written master securities lending agreement (e.g., GMSLA, MSLA)
view.s1058.req.economic_position: Lender retains risk of loss + opportunity for gain (i.e., economic ownership)
view.s1058.req.identical: IDENTICAL securities (same class, same issuer) must be returned
view.s1058.req.qualified_borrower: Borrower typically registered broker-dealer or qualifying institution
view.s1058.req.s1058_b: § 1058(b) regs add: collateral requirement, fee documentation, separate account
view.s1058.req.substitute_dividends: Substitute dividend / interest payments equal to amounts paid on borrowed securities
view.s1058.s263.applies_borrower: Borrower with appreciated stock long: capitalize substitute payments paid
view.s1058.s263.capitalize: Substitute payments paid + interest + fees CAPITALIZED to basis
view.s1058.s263.exempt: Net cost basis when offsetting position disposed
view.s1058.s263.investment_interest: Net carrying charges may convert to § 163(d) investment interest later
view.s1058.s263.no_offsetting: If no offsetting position: deductible as ordinary expense (not capitalized)
view.s1058.s263.scope: Applies to taxpayer holding offsetting position (long stock + securities loan)
view.s1058.sub.fdap: Treated as US-source FDAP for NRA withholding
view.s1058.sub.no_qualified: NOT qualified dividend income (no LTCG rate)
view.s1058.sub.s1059: § 1059 extraordinary dividend rules do NOT apply to substitutes
view.s1058.sub.s246_a_3: § 246(a)(3) holding period interruption — STARTS over when securities returned
view.s1058.sub.s263_g: § 263(g) carrying charges: capitalize if offsetting position exists
view.s1058.sub.s301_c: Compare § 301(c) actual dividend: capital + cost basis treatment
view.s1058.sub.s871m_30pct: NRA: § 871(m) 30% withholding (or treaty rate) on dividend substitutes
view.s1059.br.holding_period: Holding period of remaining stock NOT affected
view.s1059.br.nontaxed: Reduce by NON-TAXED portion = dividend × DRD %
view.s1059.br.s1059_b_below_zero: Basis reduced below $0 → excess = capital gain CURRENT year
view.s1059.br.s1059_c_calculation: For multiple stocks held, allocate basis reduction pro-rata
view.s1059.br.s245: § 245 100% DRD: dividends from less-than-20% owned domestic that is foreign-source
view.s1059.ex.dividend_stripping: "Dividend stripping" historically: buy at $100, $50 dividend with DRD, sell at $50 = $50 loss
view.s1059.ex.fmv_election: Useful when stock acquired at significant premium
view.s1059.ex.no_actual_distribution: Constructive dividends + § 305(c) deemed distributions also subject
view.s1059.ex.now_blocked: Post-§ 1059: $50 dividend × 50% DRD = $25 basis reduction → no artificial loss
view.s1059.ex.s1059_a_4_election: § 1059(a)(4) FMV election: substitute FMV basis instead of adjusted basis
view.s1059.ex.s1059_c_2: 85-day aggregation also for 365-day window if cumulative ≥ 20% basis
view.s1059.ex.s1059_e_partial_liquidatio: § 1059(e) partial liquidation: treated as extraordinary regardless of size
view.s1059.ex.s246a_no_drd: § 246A — partially debt-financed stock: reduced DRD percentage
view.s1059.ex.s246c_45day: § 246(c) 45-day holding (90 days for preferred) — to qualify for DRD
view.s1059.ex.s246d_short: § 246(d) — short sale + similar offsetting position eliminates DRD
view.s1059.ex.s301_excess: § 301(c)(2) basis recovery + § 301(c)(3) capital gain — separate timing
view.s1059.h1.title: // § 1059 EXTRAORDINARY DIVIDEND BASIS REDUCTION
view.s1059.hint.intro: Corp shareholder reduces basis by non-taxed portion (DRD) of extraordinary dividend. 5% common / 10% preferred + 85-day aggregation + 2-yr holding.
view.s1059.hp.exemption: Holding period > 2 YEARS before declaration date: § 1059 typically NOT apply
view.s1059.hp.failed_2yr: < 2-year holding: § 1059 ALWAYS applies regardless of size
view.s1059.hp.measurement: Measured from acquisition to declaration date
view.s1059.hp.qualified_purchase: Purchased post-Aug 31, 1995: post-TIPRA amendments accelerated gain rather than disposition deferral
view.s1059.hp.s246_a_2: § 246(a)(2) — actual holding period requirements for DRD eligibility (45 days)
view.s1059.rel.s162a: § 162(k) — golden parachute → ordinary deduction denied (similar anti-abuse)
view.s106.cad.aca_state: Some state-level cost-sharing taxes remain (CA, NY, MA)
view.s106.cad.deduction_unlimited: Therefore: employer health deduction remains UNLIMITED (subject to § 162 reasonableness)
view.s106.cad.future_pdf: Future: § 105(h) discrimination + non-discrimination still apply for self-insured plans
view.s106.cad.replaced: Replaced by: Medical Device Tax repeal + Cadillac tax repeal + Health Insurance Tax repeal
view.s106.cad.tcja_repealed: 40% excise tax on high-cost employer plans REPEALED by Further Consolidated Appropriations Act 2020
view.s106.cad.was_2020: Was scheduled to take effect 2020 — never enforced
view.s106.exc.2_pct: 2%+ S-corp shareholders: NOT § 106 — must include premiums on W-2 Box 1
view.s106.exc.dp_dependent: Domestic partner: § 106 if qualifies as § 152 tax dependent (income + support tests)
view.s106.exc.dp_imputed: Otherwise: domestic partner premiums → IMPUTED INCOME on W-2 Box 1 / FICA
view.s106.exc.kids_27: PPACA: kids ≤ 27 covered tax-free under § 106 (even if not tax dependent)
view.s106.exc.partners: Partners + LLC members: NOT § 106 — must include premiums on Schedule K-1
view.s106.exc.retirees: Retiree health coverage: § 106 if structured as continuing employer obligation
view.s106.exc.s162l: BUT § 162(l) above-the-line deduction available to all SE persons + 2%+ S-corp
view.s106.exc.wellness: Wellness program incentives (cash, gym): NOT § 106 — included as wages
view.s106.h1.title: // § 106 EMPLOYER HEALTH COVERAGE
view.s106.hint.intro: Employer contributions to accident / health plans EXCLUDED from employee gross income. Includes: health insurance premiums, HSA, HRA, vision / dental. 2%+ S-corp shareholders: NOT excluded — must INCLUDE on W-2 Box 1 (exception: can claim § 162(l) SE health deduction). § 125 cafeteria plan + § 106: pre-tax salary contribution. ACA mandate: employer-shared responsibility for > 50 FTE. NOT excluded: domestic partner coverage (unless dependent), wellness program incentives, gym memberships.
view.s106.hsa.cafeteria: § 125 cafeteria pre-tax: salary reduction contribution to HSA
view.s106.hsa.employer_employee: Employer + employee contributions COMBINED count to limit
view.s106.hsa.fsa_limit: FSA ≠ HSA: $3,300 FSA limit (2025); use-it-or-lose-it + carryover $660
view.s106.hsa.hdhp_required: Requires Qualified High Deductible Plan (HDHP): 2025 min deductible $1,650/$3,300; max OOP $8,300/$16,600
view.s106.hsa.investment: Investment growth: tax-free if used for qualified medical (Vanguard, Fidelity HSAs)
view.s106.hsa.limit_2025: 2025 contribution limit: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family + $1,000 catch-up age 55+
view.s106.hsa.medicare_no: Medicare enrollment ENDS HSA contributions (incl. spousal HSA)
view.s106.hsa.retirement: Post-65: withdrawal for non-medical = INCOME (no penalty); for medical = tax-free
view.s106.hsa.triple: Triple-tax-advantaged: contribution + growth + qualified medical withdrawal all tax-free
view.s106.s_corp_note: 2%+ S-corp shareholder: § 106 EXCLUSION NOT AVAILABLE. Premiums INCLUDED on W-2 Box 1. BUT claim § 162(l) above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) for SE health insurance — full income offset BUT no FICA reduction. Subject to earned-income limitation.
view.s106.scope.cobra: Employer-paid COBRA premiums for terminated employees: excluded
view.s106.scope.dental_vision: Dental + vision: included in 'accident / health' broadly
view.s106.scope.dp_excluded: Domestic partner (non-spouse): generally NOT § 106 unless tax dependent
view.s106.scope.hsa: HSA contributions (§ 223): excluded from income + FICA
view.s106.scope.long_term_care: Long-term care insurance (§ 7702B): excluded up to age limits
view.s106.scope.opposite_sex_cohabitant: Opposite-sex cohabitant: not § 106; tax dependent test applies
view.s106.scope.same_sex: Same-sex spousal coverage: excluded post-Windsor (2013) / Obergefell (2015)
view.s1092.h1.title: // § 1092 STRADDLE / MIXED-STRADDLE
view.s1092.hint.intro: Straddle = offsetting positions in actively traded property. Loss on one leg DEFERRED to extent of unrealized gain on offsetting leg. Holding period TOLLED during straddle. § 263(g): interest + carrying charges CAPITALIZED. § 1092(b)(2) Mixed-Straddle Election: identify positions to allow § 1256 60/40 treatment + non-§ 1256 leg. § 1233(d) Killer Straddle: short-against-the-box trap.
view.s1092.id.actively_traded: Actively traded personal property (stocks, options, futures, currencies)
view.s1092.id.delta: Offsetting positions with substantial reduction in risk of loss
view.s1092.id.hedging: § 1221(a)(7) hedging transactions: ordinary character, exempt from § 1092
view.s1092.id.identified_straddle: 'Identified straddle' (§ 1092(a)(2)): elect within 30 days for special treatment
view.s1092.id.presumption: Presumption: same-asset hedges (e.g., long stock + protective put)
view.s1092.id.qualified_covered: 'Qualified covered calls' (§ 1092(c)(4)) EXEMPT — OTM call against long stock
view.s1092.mixed.body: When one position is § 1256 (futures, broad-based index options) and another is not, taxpayer may elect to: (1) treat § 1256 position as non-§ 1256 (no 60/40 + no MTM), OR (2) net the positions identifying each pre-straddle. Annual election; identification within first day of straddle creation. Useful for tax-rate arbitrage between 60/40 (LT 23.8% blended max) vs full ordinary treatment.
view.s1092.rel.246: § 246(c) holding period requirement for QDI ÷ DRD
view.s119.h1.title: // § 119 MEALS + LODGING FOR EMPLOYER CONVENIENCE
view.s119.hint.intro: Excluded from employee income if furnished: (1) on business premises + (2) for convenience of employer + (3) lodging requires 'as condition of employment'. TCJA: 50% disallowance of employer DEDUCTION 2018-2025; employee exclusion preserved. Common: hotels, hospital cafeteria, on-site housing for camp managers, lighthouse keepers.
view.s119.lodging.company_subsidized: Subsidized housing without true requirement = taxable
view.s119.lodging.condition: Employee must accept as a condition of employment
view.s119.lodging.duty: Required to be on call 24/7 at facility
view.s119.lodging.remote: Remote location with no reasonable alternative housing
view.s119.lodging.security: Security / on-site management (apartment manager, lighthouse, camp)
view.s119.meals.emergency: Available for emergency calls during meal period
view.s119.meals.full_field_staff: Meals furnished for substantial non-compensatory reason
view.s119.meals.hospital: Hospital cafeteria food for medical staff (Boyd Gaming Corp)
view.s119.meals.short_lunch: Short lunch period requires staying on premises
view.s119.meals.taxable_to_employer: Pre-2026: 50% deduction limit for employer (down from 100%)
view.s119.rel.119b: § 119(b)(3): faculty housing exception ≥ 5% FMV cost or rent
view.s119.rel.132e: § 132(e) De minimis: occasional supper money + coffee
view.s119.rel.7872: § 7872 Below-market loan rules for housing assistance
view.s1202.cap.basis_advantage: 10× basis advantage: $500K basis → $5M cap from basis alone (so $10M baseline still controls)
view.s1202.cap.cumulative: Cumulative across multiple sale years (Form 8949 reporting)
view.s1202.cap.deathbed: Death: stepped-up basis terminates QSBS treatment for heirs (§ 1014)
view.s1202.cap.gift_split: Lifetime gifts: recipient inherits holding period + acquisition date + basis
view.s1202.cap.greater: Cap = GREATER of $10M (lifetime) OR 10× aggregate basis per ISSUER
view.s1202.cap.per_issuer: Cap applied per ISSUER (not per stock holding) per TAXPAYER
view.s1202.cap.stacking: Stacking with multiple beneficiaries: trust + family members each get separate cap
view.s1202.h1.title: // § 1202 QSBS GAIN EXCLUSION
view.s1202.hint.intro: Up to 100% exclusion of gain on QSBS held > 5 years. Cap: greater of $10M or 10× basis per issuer. C-corp + ≤ $50M gross assets + 80% active qualified business.
view.s1202.req.active_business: ≥ 80% (by value) used in active qualified trade or business
view.s1202.req.ccorp: Domestic C-corporation (NOT S-corp, NOT partnership, NOT LLC default)
view.s1202.req.gross_assets: ≤ $50M aggregate gross assets at issuance + IMMEDIATELY after
view.s1202.req.original_issuance: Acquired at ORIGINAL issuance (NOT secondary purchase)
view.s1202.req.qualified_business: Qualified business — excludes banking, insurance, services, farming, extraction, hospitality
view.s1202.req.real_estate_excl: Real estate held for ≥ 10% of value disqualifies
view.s1202.req.s1202_e_3: § 1202(e)(3): excluded fields = health, law, engineering, architecture, accounting, actuarial science, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial services, brokerage, "reputation/skill" principal asset
view.s1202.req.s1202_e_5: § 1202(e)(5): no more than 10% of value in stock or securities of unrelated corporations
view.s1202.s1045.basis_carryover: Basis = cost of replacement reduced by deferred gain
view.s1202.s1045.election: Election made on Schedule D + Form 8949
view.s1202.s1045.holding_carryover: Holding period carries over to replacement
view.s1202.s1045.individuals: Individuals only — NOT corporations or partnerships at entity level
view.s1202.s1045.preserves_qsbs: Preserves QSBS character + cumulative holding for eventual § 1202 sale
view.s1202.s1045.purpose: Defer gain on QSBS sale by reinvesting in another QSBS
view.s1202.strat.dynasty: Dynasty trust: pass to grandchildren skipping § 1202 cap exhaustion
view.s1202.strat.exchanges_pre: § 351 stock-for-stock exchange may continue QSBS clock
view.s1202.strat.f_reorg: F reorganization can preserve QSBS — careful planning
view.s1202.strat.gift: Lifetime gift to trust / family: each donee gets separate $10M cap
view.s1202.strat.qbsg: Qualified small business growing: convert to C-corp BEFORE valuation > $50M
view.s1202.strat.s1202h: § 1202(h) gift/inheritance — donee tacks holding period + basis
view.s1202.strat.s83b: § 83(b) election: tacks holding period for restricted stock
view.s1202.strat.timing: 5-year clock starts: ORIGINAL issuance date of QSBS
view.s1202.strat.trust: Multiple irrevocable trusts ("QSBS multipliers"): each trust = separate cap
view.s121.h1.title: // § 121 ΑΠΟΚΛΕΙΣΜΟΣ ΚΥΡΙΑΣ ΚΑΤΟΙΚΙΑΣ
view.s121.hint.intro: 250.000 $ single / $500.000 MFJ εξαιρούνται από το κέρδος από την πώληση της κύριας κατοικίας. Δοκιμή ιδιοκτησίας: ≥ 2 από τα τελευταία 5 χρόνια. Χρήση δοκιμής: ≥ 2 των τελευταίων 5 ετών ως κύρια κατοικία. Μία φορά ανά 2 χρόνια. Μετά το 2008 ΜΗ ΕΙΔΙΚΕΥΜΕΝΗ ΧΡΗΣΗ (ενοικιάσεις, διακοπές) κατανεμήθηκε αναλογικά. Εργασία/υγεία/απρόβλεπτη περίσταση: μερικός αποκλεισμός (μήνες ÷ 24).
view.s121.label.months_qualifying: Μήνες που έχουν πραγματικά χρησιμοποιηθεί / ιδιοκτησία (αν είναι μερική)
view.s121.spec.dep_recapture: § 1250 μη ανακτηθείσα ανακατάληψη ΕΞΑΙΡΕΤΑΙ από την § 121 — φορολογείται με μέγιστο 25%
view.s121.spec.disabled: Η φροντίδα των εγκαταστάσεων για άτομα με ειδικές ανάγκες υπολογίζεται ως χρήση κατοικίας
view.s121.spec.divorce: Διαζύγιο: χρονομετρήσεις σύμφωνα με την § 121(d)(3) για τον μεταγραφόμενο σύζυγο
view.s121.spec.military: Στρατιωτικοί / Υπηρεσίες Εξωτερικών / Intel: περίοδος αναστολής 10 ετών
view.s121.spec.no_capital_loss: Προσωπική κατοικία: § 165 ΟΧΙ εκπίπτουσα απώλεια (μόνο επένδυση / ενοικίαση)
view.s121.spec.widow: Επιζών σύζυγος: Ο αποκλεισμός MFJ 500.000 $ διατηρείται εάν πωληθεί εντός 2 ετών από τον θάνατο του συζύγου
view.s1212.h1.title: // § 1212 CAP LOSS CARRYFORWARD
view.s1212.hint.intro: Individuals: $3,000/yr ordinary-income offset ($1,500 MFS); excess CARRIES FORWARD INDEFINITELY. Character (ST/LT) preserved. C-corps: 3-yr CARRYBACK + 5-yr CARRYFORWARD, ALL ST character. Death of taxpayer: unused carryforward LOST (doesn't pass to estate). § 475(f) trader MTM: ordinary loss instead — no $3k cap, no 5-yr limit (corp). Bankruptcy estate: uses Code § 1398 separate filing.
view.s1212.net.absorb_st_first: $3k absorbs ST loss FIRST (ST taxed at ordinary rate anyway)
view.s1212.net.character_preserved: Character preserved going forward (ST remains ST)
view.s1212.net.cross_class: Net ST excess loss against LT gain (or vice versa)
view.s1212.net.ordinary_offset: Excess net loss offsets ordinary income up to $3k ($1,500 MFS)
view.s1212.net.same_class: Net ST gains × ST losses; net LT gains × LT losses
view.s1212.note.large_carry: Large carryforward: consider § 475(f) MTM election if TTS-qualified to convert to ordinary loss without $3k cap. Roth conversions in low-income years consume carryforward at favorable rates.
view.s1212.spec.amt: AMT capital loss carryforward separately tracked
view.s1212.spec.bankruptcy: Bankruptcy estate: § 1398 + estate uses individual's CF separately
view.s1212.spec.death: Death of taxpayer: carryforward LOST entirely (not estate, not heirs)
view.s1212.spec.divorce: Divorce: each spouse gets own share of joint carryforward (Rev. Rul. 71-382)
view.s1212.spec.section_1296: PFIC § 1296 MTM losses limited to prior MTM gains; not regular cap loss
view.s1212.spec.section_382: § 382 ownership change limits corp capital loss carryforward
view.s1231.h1.title: // § 1231 BUSINESS PROPERTY (HOTCHPOT)
view.s1231.hint.intro: Net § 1231 gain = LTCG. Net § 1231 loss = ordinary. § 1231(c) 5-yr look-back recaptures gain as ordinary up to cumulative losses. § 1245/§ 1250 applied first.
view.s1231.hp.character: Character determined ENTITY-LEVEL at partnership / S-corp
view.s1231.hp.firepot_first: "Firepot" sub-netting: net casualty losses + involuntary conversions
view.s1231.hp.firepot_gain: If firepot is NET GAIN → enters main hotchpot for LTCG/loss netting
view.s1231.hp.firepot_loss: If firepot is NET LOSS → goes to ordinary (Schedule A or Schedule C)
view.s1231.hp.s1245_first: § 1245 / § 1250 recapture applied FIRST — only excess enters hotchpot
view.s1231.hp.step1: Step 1: Net all § 1231 gains and losses for the year
view.s1231.hp.step2: Step 2: If net is GAIN → LTCG (subject to look-back recapture)
view.s1231.hp.step3: Step 3: If net is LOSS → ordinary loss (Schedule D, line 1)
view.s1231.lb.5yr: Looks back 5 most recent tax years
view.s1231.lb.cumulative: Cumulative net § 1231 losses in those 5 years
view.s1231.lb.example: Example: prior 5 yrs $50K cumulative loss + current $80K gain → $50K ordinary + $30K LTCG
view.s1231.lb.individual_partnership: Tested at INDIVIDUAL partner level (not partnership)
view.s1231.lb.no_carryover: Recaptured losses do NOT carryforward — used up in current year
view.s1231.lb.purpose: Anti-abuse: prevents alternating loss + gain years to game character
view.s1231.lb.recapture: Current year § 1231 GAIN recaptured as ORDINARY up to cumulative losses
view.s1231.prop.crops: Unharvested crops on land sold simultaneously
view.s1231.prop.depreciable: Depreciable property used in trade or business — held > 1 year
view.s1231.prop.real_estate: Real property used in trade or business — held > 1 year
view.s1231.spec.casualty: Casualty / theft losses on personal-use property: separate treatment
view.s1231.spec.partnership: Partnership: § 1231 character flows through to partners
view.s1231.spec.s1239: § 1239 transfer to controlled entity: ordinary recapture
view.s1231.spec.s1245_complete: § 1245 recapture is COMPLETE — no § 1231 if full recapture
view.s1231.tbl.unrec_s1250: Unrecaptured § 1250 gain split out for 25% rate
view.s1233.h1.title: // § 1233 SHORT SALE HOLDING PERIOD
view.s1233.hint.intro: Short sale gain: holding period determined by stock HELD at time of short, not closing. Loss on short: long-term if > 1 yr; otherwise short-term. 'Short-against-the-box' or 'covered short': holding identical stock long before short → § 1233(b) suspends OR resets holding period of long. § 1259 constructive sale takes precedence for substantially-eliminated risk.
view.s1233.label.long_held_at_short: Held same stock long at short date?
view.s1233.label.long_more_year: Long stock held > 1 yr at short?
view.s1233.rule.box_disposes: Closing the short 'against the box' = sells the LONG (taxable disposition)
view.s1233.rule.gain_simple: Pure short with no long held: gain ALWAYS short-term (you owned the borrowed share < 1 yr)
view.s1233.rule.identical: 'Substantially identical' = same stock, same class, similar economic exposure
view.s1233.rule.long_held_over_yr: Long held > 1 yr + short = LT character protected for long; short still ST
view.s1233.rule.long_held_under_yr: Long held < 1 yr + short = long holding period RESTARTS at short close
view.s1233.rule.loss_holdings: Loss on close: looks at how long you owned the SOLD stock (the close buy)
view.s1234.h.character: Option in/on capital asset → option gain/loss is CAPITAL
view.s1234.h.exercise_basis: Exercise: premium added to basis of underlying acquired
view.s1234.h.exercise_no_gain: No gain/loss at exercise — built into underlying's basis
view.s1234.h.holding_starts_separately: Holding period for underlying STARTS at exercise — does NOT tack to option period
view.s1234.h.lapse_loss: Lapse without exercise: capital LOSS treated as if sale for $0 (Reg § 1.1234-1(b))
view.s1234.h.put_exercise: Put exercise (sale of underlying): premium REDUCES sale proceeds
view.s1234.h.s1234_a_3: § 1234(a)(3) special holding period rule for cash-settled options on capital assets
view.s1234.h.sale: Sale of option: ordinary gain/loss UNLESS § 1234(a) applies (capital)
view.s1234.h1.title: // § 1234 OPTIONS CHARACTER
view.s1234.hint.intro: Holder gain/loss on capital underlying = capital. Writer: sale = STCG; LAPSE = ordinary. § 1256 60/40 + MTM for non-equity options + broad index.
view.s1234.s1256.mtm: Mark-to-market at year-end: deemed sold at FMV Dec 31
view.s1234.s1256.qualified_board: Must trade on qualified board / exchange (CFTC-regulated typically)
view.s1234.s1256.s1256_d: § 1256(d) hedging exception: bona fide hedge → ordinary character
view.s1234.s1256.split: 60% LONG-term + 40% SHORT-term regardless of actual holding
view.s1234.s1256.spx: SPX, SPY ARE different: SPY (single-stock ETF) NOT § 1256; SPX (index option) IS § 1256
view.s1234.s1256.types: Non-equity options, broad-based stock indices, regulated futures, foreign currency contracts
view.s1234.spec.bond_call: Bond call/put features: § 1271(a) interest treatment, not § 1234
view.s1234.spec.lap: LEAP (Long-term Equity AnticiPation Securities) — 1+ year expiry, same § 1234 treatment
view.s1234.spec.s1233_short: § 1233 short sales: holding period rules differ for matched positions
view.s1234.spec.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale — short-against-box + similar offsetting positions
view.s1234.spec.warrant: Warrants: § 1234 applies; cost basis adjusts by premium
view.s1234.str.defined: "Straddle" = offsetting positions in personal property (e.g., long stock + put)
view.s1234.str.identified: Identified straddle: special set of rules — basis adjustments
view.s1234.str.s1092_a: § 1092(a) — loss deferral: loss recognized only to extent NOT offset by unrecognized gain
view.s1234.str.s1092_b: § 1092(b) — capitalization: interest/carrying charges capitalized to basis
view.s1234.str.s1092_c_qcc: § 1092(c)(4)(A) — Qualified Covered Call exception (limited stocks + LT positions)
view.s1234.str.s1234_b_holding_term: Straddle terminates LT holding period of long leg (rolls back to date of offsetting position)
view.s1234.str.s263_g: § 263(g) — capitalize carrying charges on straddle positions
view.s1234.w.assignment_call: Assignment of call: writer's basis in underlying + premium = adjusted sale price
view.s1234.w.assignment_put: Assignment of put: writer's basis in underlying = strike - premium received
view.s1234.w.close_out: Close-out: STCG/STCL (writer's position generally short-term character)
view.s1234.w.lapse: Lapse with premium retained: ORDINARY income (premium received, no offsetting outflow)
view.s1234.w.s1234_b_repeal: § 1234(b) special lapse rule applies BEFORE § 1234A general termination rule
view.s1234.w.sale: Sale or exchange by writer: SHORT-TERM CAPITAL gain/loss
view.s1239.applies_note: § 1239 forces ORDINARY rate (up to 37%) instead of LTCG (20%). Loss of preferential rate on gain. To avoid: sell to UNRELATED buyer (may include LLC where ownership reduced < 50%), OR distribute property to shareholder first then sale outside related-party rules.
view.s1239.coord.character_election: No election to opt-out — § 1239 mandatory if conditions met
view.s1239.coord.installment: § 453 installment method: § 1239 applies to FULL gain even if reported via installment
view.s1239.coord.no_loss: § 1239 doesn't apply to LOSSES — losses limited / disallowed by § 267 instead
view.s1239.coord.s1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange: § 1239 holding period rules — must hold 2+ yrs after exchange to avoid
view.s1239.coord.s1245: § 1245 recapture: ordinary up to depreciation; § 1239 stacks AFTER recapture
view.s1239.coord.s1250: § 1250 recapture (real estate): also ordinary; § 1239 stacks on top of any remaining cap gain
view.s1239.coord.s267: § 267 disallows LOSS on related-party sale (separate from § 1239 ordinary characterization)
view.s1239.coord.s453_2yr: § 453 2-yr resale rule: related buyer can't resell within 2 yrs to outsiders without acceleration
view.s1239.ex.basis_to_buyer: Buyer's basis = purchase price (full step-up for depreciation purposes)
view.s1239.ex.corp_subsidiary: Parent sells building to 60% subsidiary → § 1239 ordinary on gain
view.s1239.ex.family: Father sells rental property (depreciable in son's hands) to son → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.individual_corp: Individual sells equipment (capital asset to her) to her 100% S-corp → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.investment_to_personal: Sale to spouse for personal use (not depreciable) → § 1239 N/A
view.s1239.ex.no_depreciation: Sale of land (NOT depreciable) to controlled entity → § 1239 N/A — LTCG OK
view.s1239.ex.partnership_partner: Partnership sells truck to controlling partner → § 1239 ordinary
view.s1239.ex.s_corp_owners: Two S-corps with same 100% owner sell to each other → both § 1239 + § 267
view.s1239.h1.title: // § 1239 RELATED PARTY SALE
view.s1239.hint.intro: Gain on sale to RELATED PARTY = ORDINARY INCOME (not capital gain). Property must be DEPRECIABLE in hands of recipient (not seller). Related person: > 50% controlled entity, family members, partnerships, trusts, controlled corps. Purpose: prevents conversion of ordinary income (depreciation recapture eligible) to LTCG via sale to controlled buyer who then takes new depreciation basis. Always recharacterize as ordinary. Form 4797 + Schedule D.
view.s1239.label.capital: Held as capital asset by seller?
view.s1239.rel.constructive_267: § 267 constructive ownership rules apply (family + entity + options)
view.s1239.rel.corp_corp: Corporation + corporation: members of controlled group (> 50% common ownership)
view.s1239.rel.family_def: Family: spouse, ancestors, lineal descendants, siblings (full / half blood)
view.s1239.rel.partnership_ind: Partnership + partner: any partner having any partnership interest
view.s1239.rel.trust_grantor: Trust + grantor: any trust where grantor / beneficiary related
view.s1244.h1.title: // § 1244 ΤΑΚΤΙΚΗ ΑΠΩΛΕΙΑ ΣΤΟ SBS
view.s1244.hint.intro: Μετατρέψτε την απώλεια κεφαλαίου σε ΤΑΚΤΙΚΗ ζημία έως και 50.000 $ μεμονωμένα / $100.000 MFJ όταν η μετοχή της μικρής επιχείρησης πηγαίνει νότια. Το απόθεμα πρέπει να ανήκει σε εγχώρια εταιρεία με συγκεντρωμένο συνολικό κεφάλαιο 1 εκατομμυρίου $ (σωρευτικά). Πρέπει να είστε το ΠΡΩΤΟΤΥΠΟ ΖΗΤΗΜΑ και όχι ο εκδοχέας. Σημαντική διάσωση για αποτυχημένους επενδυτές startup.
view.s1244.label.corp_capital: Συνολικό εταιρικό κεφάλαιο που αντλήθηκε κατά την έκδοση ($)
view.s1244.qual.aggregate: Συγκεντρωμένο εταιρικό κεφάλαιο που συγκεντρώθηκε κατά την έκδοση ≤ $1.000.000
view.s1244.qual.domestic: Εγχώρια C-corp ή S-corp (όχι LLC, όχι ξένη)
view.s1244.qual.individual: Μόνο άτομα + συνεργασίες πληρούν τις προϋποθέσεις (όχι εταιρείες, όχι καταπιστεύματα)
view.s1244.qual.issuee: Πρέπει να είστε το ΠΡΩΤΟΤΥΠΟ ΤΕΥΧΟΣ (που αποκτήθηκε με αντάλλαγμα χρήματα ή περιουσία, ΟΧΙ υπηρεσίες)
view.s1244.qual.no_swap: ΔΕΝ αποκτήθηκε μέσω αφορολόγητου reorg / εισφοράς
view.s1244.qual.passive: < 50% των ακαθάριστων εσόδων από παθητικές πηγές (royalties, ενοίκια, μερίσματα, τόκοι, υπεραξία) τα προηγούμενα 5 χρόνια
view.s1244.warning.fails: ΔΕΝ πληροί τις προϋποθέσεις. Η απώλεια είναι κεφάλαιο, που υπόκειται σε συμψηφισμό 3.000 $/έτος.
view.s1245.calc.depreciation: Depreciation recapture potential = ALL depreciation (incl § 179 + bonus + § 168 MACRS)
view.s1245.calc.excess: Excess gain = § 1231 gain (LTCG if net § 1231 gain)
view.s1245.calc.full_recap: Full recapture if cost > adjusted basis (i.e., depreciation taken)
view.s1245.calc.ordinary: Recapture amount taxed as ORDINARY INCOME
view.s1245.calc.realized: Realized gain = sale proceeds − adjusted basis
view.s1245.calc.recapture: Recapture amount = LESSER of (gain) OR (recapture potential)
view.s1245.calc.s291: § 291 corporate add'l recapture: 20% × (§ 1250 amount that would be § 1245 recapture)
view.s1245.cat.a3b: § 1245(a)(3)(B) — other tangible property used in trade/business (storage facilities)
view.s1245.cat.livestock: Livestock held for breeding / dairy / draft (§ 1245(a)(3)(A) per Treas. Reg.)
view.s1245.cat.s197: § 197 amortizable intangibles (goodwill, going concern, customer lists, etc.)
view.s1245.h1.title: // § 1245 DEPRECIATION RECAPTURE
view.s1245.hint.intro: Recharacterizes gain as ordinary income to extent of accumulated depreciation. Recapture = lesser of (gain) OR (depreciation). Excess = § 1231 capital gain.
view.s1245.int.contrast_pre197: Pre-§ 197 (pre-Aug 10, 1993): goodwill not amortizable, capital gain on sale
view.s1245.int.gain_ordinary: Sale of goodwill: full § 1245 recapture as ORDINARY income
view.s1245.int.no_capital: No capital gain treatment for amortized portion
view.s1245.int.partial_sale: Partial sale of asset group: recapture allocated by FMV (Reg § 1.1245-1)
view.s1245.int.s1060: § 1060 residual method allocates basis among classes (Class VI = goodwill)
view.s1245.int.s197_15yr: § 197 amortization 15 years straight-line on goodwill, going concern, etc.
view.s1245.plan.1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange DEFERS recapture (real property only post-TCJA)
view.s1245.plan.charitable: Gift of depreciated property: reduce basis by acc. depr; recipient inherits
view.s1245.plan.cost_seg: Cost segregation: more depreciation now, more § 1245 recapture later — net positive
view.s1245.plan.death: Step-up at death (§ 1014): recapture WIPED OUT — heir gets fresh basis
view.s1245.plan.installment: § 453 installment sale: § 1245 recapture all RECOGNIZED in year of sale (no deferral)
view.s1245.spec.casualty_destruction: Casualty: § 1245(b)(4) — gain to extent insurance > basis triggers recapture
view.s1245.spec.cost_segregation: Cost segregation study: identifies § 1245 vs § 1250 split — accelerates depreciation but creates recapture exposure
view.s1245.spec.death: Death: § 1245(b)(2) — basis stepped up; recapture potential extinguished
view.s1245.spec.gift: Gift: § 1245(b)(1) — recapture potential carries over to donee
view.s1245.spec.installment: § 453 installment sale: recapture in YEAR 1 — pulls forward all ordinary income
view.s1245.spec.like_kind: § 1031 like-kind: TCJA limits to real property; § 1245 personal property NO LONGER ELIGIBLE
view.s1245.spec.s1033: § 1033 replacement: defer gain; recapture potential preserved in replacement property
view.s1245.spec.s1250_real_property: Cost seg-identified § 1245 components from real estate trigger recapture on sale
view.s1248.app.cfc: Foreign corp was CFC at any time during 5-year period before sale
view.s1248.app.controlled_chain: Sale of foreign corp holding CFCs: chain attribution to lower-tier CFC E&P
view.s1248.app.gain: Recharacterize gain up to allocated E&P (current-yr inclusion + accumulated)
view.s1248.app.ptep: PTEP / PTI excluded — already-taxed amounts not re-taxed
view.s1248.app.shareholder: US 10% shareholder (vote OR value) sells CFC stock
view.s1248.app.shareholder_during: Seller was US 10% shareholder at any time during 5-year period
view.s1248.h1.title: // § 1248 CFC STOCK SALE
view.s1248.hint.intro: US 10% shareholder selling CFC stock: gain up to allocated foreign E&P recharacterized as DIVIDEND. Post-2017 PTEP (previously taxed E&P from GILTI / Subpart F) excluded — already taxed. C-corp shareholder + § 245A 100% DRD: recharacterized portion exempt → tax-free repatriation via sale. Individual: qualified dividend rate (20%) if treaty country + holding period. § 1248(f) also reaches outbound transfers (§ 367(b)). Form 5471 Sch P + § 1248 worksheet.
view.s1248.plan.high_tax: High-tax accumulated E&P: better creditability post-recharacterization
view.s1248.plan.holding: Holding period: 365 days (§ 246(c) for § 245A) — plan acquisition timing
view.s1248.plan.individual_treaty: Individual: qualified dividend if treaty country + 60-day hold (otherwise ordinary)
view.s1248.plan.ptep_first: PTEP-first accounting: deplete PTEP before § 1248 recharacterization
view.s1248.plan.s245a: C-corp seller + § 245A: 100% DRD on recharacterized portion → tax-free
view.s1248.plan.s367b: § 367(b) outbound: similar mechanism on outbound transfers
view.s1248.plan.subF_purification: Pre-sale 'purification': dividend out subF / GILTI accumulated E&P
view.s1248.s245a_note: § 245A 100% DRD applied to recharacterized portion. Result: tax-free repatriation of CFC E&P via stock sale. Remaining capital gain still taxed at 20%. Combined with high-tax GILTI exclusion + PTEP basis adjustments, this is the optimal exit path for US C-corps.
view.s125.h1.title: // § 125 CAFETERIA PLAN
view.s125.hint.intro: Allows employees to CHOOSE between cash + qualified benefits without constructive receipt. Common: pre-tax health insurance, Health FSA ($3,200/yr), Dependent Care FSA ($5,000), HSA contributions. Salary reduction reduces both income tax + FICA. Non-discrimination tests: eligibility, contribution + benefits, key employee concentration. Written plan + irrevocable elections (except qualifying status changes).
view.s125.nd.contributions: Contributions + benefits: comparable terms for all participants
view.s125.nd.eligibility: Eligibility: no group of HCEs treated more favorably for participation
view.s125.nd.failed: Failed: HCEs lose tax-favored treatment for full year (recharacterized income)
view.s125.nd.key_employee: Key employee concentration: ≤ 25% of nontaxable benefits go to key employees
view.s125.nd.simple_cafeteria: Simple cafeteria plan (≤ 100 employees): safe harbor passes all tests automatically
view.s125.nd.testing_date: Annual testing on last day of plan year
view.s125.qb.401k: 401(k) contributions (pre-tax employee deferrals; usually separate plan but can interact)
view.s125.qb.fsa: Health FSA: $3,200 (2024), $640 carryover, 2.5 mo grace period
view.s125.sc.dependent: Dependent satisfies / ceases to satisfy eligibility
view.s125.sc.employment: Change in employment status (you / spouse / dependent)
view.s125.sc.must_be_consistent: Change must be consistent with status event
view.s1250.h1.title: // § 1250 REAL PROPERTY RECAPTURE
view.s1250.hint.intro: Recaptures excess over straight-line as ordinary. Post-1986 SL only → effectively zero. Unrecaptured § 1250 gain taxed at 25% max. § 291 adds 20% for C-corps.
view.s1250.s291.amount: 20% × amount that WOULD be § 1245 recapture if § 1245 applied
view.s1250.s291.book_tax: Book vs tax difference — § 291 creates temporary BTD
view.s1250.s291.corp_only: C-corporations only — not individuals or S-corps
view.s1250.s291.effective_rate: Effective: 21% × 20% = 4.2% additional federal tax on corporate sales
view.s1250.s291.ordinary: Treated as additional ordinary income (not capital gain)
view.s1250.strat.charitable_remainder: CRT split-interest: defer gain over income payment period
view.s1250.strat.cost_seg: Cost segregation accelerates dep (15-yr) but creates § 1245 recapture exposure
view.s1250.strat.death: § 1014 stepped-up basis at death extinguishes unrealized recapture
view.s1250.strat.gifts: § 1015 carryover basis on gift preserves recapture potential
view.s1250.strat.opportunity_zone: QOZ deferral § 1400Z-2 — basis step-up after 10 years
view.s1250.strat.partnership: Hold through partnership: § 743(b) step-up may reset for new partners
view.s1250.strat.s1031: § 1031 like-kind exchange defers gain + unrecaptured § 1250 gain
view.s1250.strat.s1033: § 1033 involuntary conversion defers gain + recapture potential preserved
view.s1250.strat.s453: § 453 installment sale spreads recognition across receipts
view.s1250.unr.amount: Amount = post-1986 straight-line depreciation that ALREADY claimed
view.s1250.unr.calculation: = LESSER of (depreciation taken) OR (gain on sale)
view.s1250.unr.individuals: Individuals only — corporations taxed at flat 21% anyway
view.s1250.unr.netting: Netted against capital losses BEFORE applying rate (Schedule D Worksheet)
view.s1250.unr.niit: § 1411 NIIT 3.8% adds on top → effective 28.8% max
view.s1250.unr.rate: Taxed at MAXIMUM 25% — vs 15%/20% LTCG, vs 37% ordinary
view.s1250.unr.recapture_total: Total tax burden on long-held real estate disposition can be significant
view.s1250.unr.s_residential: Residential rental 27.5 yr: $100K depreciation after 27.5 yrs full
view.s1256.h1.title: // ΤΜΗΜΑ 1256 60/40
view.s1256.hint.intro: Futures + SPX/NDX/VIX options + forwards: 60% μακροπρόθεσμα / 40% βραχυπρόθεσμα ανεξάρτητα από την περίοδο διακράτησης. Mark-to-market στο τέλος του έτους. Η καθαρή απώλεια § 1256 μπορεί να μεταφερθεί ΠΙΣΩ 3 χρόνια (μοναδικό όφελος έναντι κανονικής απώλειας κεφαλαίου).
view.s1256.loss_carryback: Καθαρή ΑΠΩΛΕΙΑ § 1256 — μπορεί να μεταφέρει πίσω έως και 3 χρόνια για να αντισταθμίσει προηγούμενα κέρδη § 1256. Φόρμα αρχείου 6781 + τροποποίηση προηγούμενων επιστροφών (Έντυπο 1040-X). Το Carryforward είναι επίσης εντάξει.
view.s1259.h1.title: // § 1259 CONSTRUCTIVE SALE
view.s1259.hint.intro: Anti-short-against-the-box rule. If you hold an appreciated long position and enter into a hedging transaction that substantially eliminates risk + gain opportunity — treated as SOLD at FMV on that date. Cap gain recognized immediately. Defeats deferral strategies. Common triggers: short-against-the-box, equity swap pair, forward sale at fixed price, collar with overlap.
view.s1259.safe.atm_collar: ATM collars + protective puts generally don't trigger (gain opportunity remains)
view.s1259.safe.both_required: BOTH conditions required for safe-harbor protection
view.s1259.safe.close_by_jan30: Position closed within 30 days after year-end
view.s1259.safe.delta_hedge: Variable forward / VPF: usually safe IF retain delta > 0.20
view.s1259.safe.long_held_60: Long position held 60+ days after close of identifying transaction without diminished risk
view.s1259.work.collar: Wide-strike collar maintains real risk + gain opportunity
view.s1259.work.exchange_fund: Exchange fund: 7-year hold for diversification without constructive sale
view.s1259.work.margin_loan: Margin loan against unhedged position (no constructive sale)
view.s1259.work.protective_put: Pure protective put: full upside retained, downside hedged
view.s1259.work.vpf: Variable Prepaid Forward (VPF): give up upside in narrow band, keep some risk
view.s127.h1.title: // § 127 EDUCATIONAL ASSISTANCE
view.s127.hint.intro: Employer can provide up to $5,250/year tax-free for qualifying education. Through 12/31/2025: includes employer payments toward student loans (SECURE 2.0 / CARES Act). Excludes sports, games, hobbies (unless job-related). Non-discrimination + written plan required. Above $5,250 generally taxable unless qualifies as 'working condition fringe' § 132(d).
view.s127.nd.eligibility: Eligibility must not favor highly compensated
view.s127.nd.no_choice: No choice between cash + benefit (or it becomes salary)
view.s127.nd.no_more_5pct: No more than 5% can go to > 5% owners + relatives
view.s127.nd.notice: Reasonable notice to employees about plan benefits
view.s127.nd.section_117: § 117 qualified scholarship overlap allowed (tuition reduction for university employees)
view.s127.nd.section_132_overlap: Above $5,250 can be excluded under § 132(d) working condition if job-related
view.s127.nd.written_plan: Written plan required: must be reasonable + reduce taxes
view.s127.qual.related_no_required: Job-related NOT required (broader than § 132(d) working condition)
view.s127.qual.room_board_no: NOT room + board / transportation / personal use property
view.s127.qual.student_loan_2026: Student loan payments through 12/31/2025 (subject to congressional extension)
view.s127.qual.tuition: Tuition + fees at any educational institution
view.s127.rel.117: § 117 Qualified Scholarship: tax-free if degree candidate + tuition portion
view.s127.rel.132d: § 132(d) Working Condition: job-related education (no $ limit, but must be necessary)
view.s127.rel.221: § 221 Student Loan Interest deduction: $2,500/yr cap, MAGI phase-out
view.s127.rel.25A: § 25A AOTC / LLC: education credits for taxpayer's own education
view.s1273.comp.daily: Daily accrual: prorate the period accrual
view.s1273.comp.partial_year: Partial-year holding: prorate based on days held
view.s1273.comp.step1: Step 1: Find Yield to Maturity (YTM) that makes PV of cash flows = issue price
view.s1273.comp.step2: Step 2: Period accrual = (adjusted issue price × YTM) − stated interest
view.s1273.comp.step3: Step 3: Adjusted issue price t+1 = adjusted issue price t + OID accrual
view.s1273.comp.step4: Step 4: Cumulative accrual = sum of all period accruals
view.s1273.comp.step5: Step 5: Holder's basis = original cost + cumulative OID accrual
view.s1273.comp.zero_coupon: Zero coupon: stated interest = 0; all accrual is OID
view.s1273.dm.below: If actual OID < de minimis → NOT treated as OID
view.s1273.dm.character: Below-threshold: gain on sale = capital gain (not ordinary OID)
view.s1273.dm.example: Example: $1,000 par × 0.25% × 10 yrs = $25 de minimis threshold
view.s1273.dm.formula: De minimis = stated redemption × 0.25% × years to maturity
view.s1273.dm.practical: Practical: short-maturity bonds rarely have significant OID
view.s1273.dm.purpose: Purpose: prevent OID complexity on small discounts
view.s1273.h1.title: // § 1273 ORIGINAL ISSUE DISCOUNT
view.s1273.hint.intro: OID = Stated Redemption Price at Maturity − Issue Price. Accrued ratably over life of bond → INCLUDED IN INCOME each year (even WITHOUT cash receipt — zero coupon problem). De minimis test: OID < 0.25% × years to maturity → not OID. Constant yield method standard (compound basis, like loan amortization). Form 1099-OID reporting if accrual ≥ $10. Tax-exempt municipal OID: still accrued but tax-free. § 1278 market discount separate concept (post-issuance).
view.s1273.spec.aha: Applicable High-Yield Discount Obligation: > 5 yrs maturity + > AFR + 5%
view.s1273.spec.distress: Distressed debt: deep discount may be OID, not market discount
view.s1273.spec.high_yield_oid: High Yield OID Discount Obligations § 163(e)(5): payer deduction limited
view.s1273.spec.modification: § 1.1001-3: significant modification of debt = deemed new instrument with new OID
view.s1273.spec.short_term: Short-term obligations ≤ 1 yr: no OID accrual; gain at sale = ordinary
view.s1273.spec.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt OID: accrue normally but income excluded (Box 11 of 1099-OID)
view.s1273.spec.treasury_oid: US Treasury OID: state tax exempt (Box 8 of 1099-OID)
view.s1273.spec.zero_coupon_strips: Zero-coupon Treasury STRIPS: substantial annual OID accrual problem
view.s1273.zero_coupon_note: ZERO COUPON BOND: substantial annual OID accrual without cash receipts. Pay tax NOW on income you HAVEN'T received. Common problem for retail investors. Consider holding zero-coupon bonds in tax-deferred accounts (IRA, 401(k)) OR tax-exempt accounts (Roth IRA). Tax-exempt municipal zero-coupons (e.g., Build America Bonds, BABs): solved this issue.
view.s1276.h1.title: // § 1276 MARKET DISCOUNT
view.s1276.hint.intro: Buy bond below face value: accrued discount = ORDINARY income, not cap gain. De minimis safe harbor: < 0.25% × years to maturity. Accrual method: ratable OR constant-yield. § 1278(b) election: include currently → reduce gain at sale. Tax-exempt muni bonds: market discount also ordinary income (federal), but not always state. Reported on 1099-OID or 1099-INT.
view.s1276.rel.acquisition: § 1278(a)(2)(B) Acquisition Premium: amortize against OID accrual
view.s1276.rel.amortizable: Bond Premium § 171: paid > face = elect to amortize (offset interest)
view.s1276.rel.contingent: Contingent payment debt: Reg § 1.1275-4 OID-like accrual
view.s1276.rel.oid: Original Issue Discount (§ 1271-1275): bonds issued below par; OID accrues as interest
view.s1276.rel.short_term: Short-term (≤ 1 yr maturity): § 1281 mandatory current accrual
view.s129.fsa.cares_act_2020: CARES Act 2020 + ARPA 2021 temporary flexibility (carryover allowed for 2020/2021)
view.s129.fsa.employer_match: Some employers contribute additional dep care benefit on top
view.s129.fsa.no_run_out: No "run-out" period in following year for dep care (vs health FSA)
view.s129.fsa.qualifying_event: Mid-year change: only with qualifying life event (birth, marriage, divorce)
view.s129.fsa.reimbursement: Reimbursement only after expense incurred + receipts submitted
view.s129.fsa.s125: § 125 cafeteria plan election irrevocable for year
view.s129.h1.title: // § 129 DEPENDENT CARE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
view.s129.hint.intro: Employer DCAP up to $5,000 ($2,500 MFS) excluded from wages. § 125 cafeteria plan. § 21 credit coordination — no double dipping.
view.s129.nd.benefits_55_5: § 129(d)(3): benefits to 5% owners cannot exceed 25% of total
view.s129.nd.eligibility: § 129(d)(2) eligibility: not in favor of HCEs / 5% owners
view.s129.nd.failure: Failure: HCE benefits become TAXABLE (non-HCEs preserved exclusion)
view.s129.nd.reasonable: § 129(d)(4): reasonable communication to all eligible employees
view.s129.nd.s125_separate: § 125 cafeteria plan nondiscrimination separate (key employee + average benefit)
view.s129.nd.safe_harbor: § 129(d)(6): safe harbor for plans satisfying ratio + average benefits
view.s129.nd.testing_year: Annual testing — failure assessed at YEAR END
view.s129.nd.utilization: § 129(d)(5): average benefits to non-HCEs ≥ 55% of HCE average
view.s129.q.day_camp: Day camp: yes (residential overnight camp: no)
view.s129.q.in_home: In-home care: babysitter, nanny, day care provider
view.s129.q.live_together: Qualifying individual must live with taxpayer > ½ year
view.s129.q.preschool: Preschool / nursery school (educational excluded if kindergarten or higher)
view.s129.q.school_tuition: School tuition for kindergarten + above: NOT qualifying
view.s129.q.under_13: Child under 13 at time of care
view.s129.q.work_related: Must be WORK-RELATED (enable taxpayer + spouse to work)
view.s129.s21.arpa: ARPA 2021 expanded § 21 to refundable + larger — expired
view.s129.s21.dcap_first: Strategy: § 129 DCAP exclusion first (saves 22-37% income + FICA ~7.65%), then § 21 credit on remainder
view.s129.s21.limits: § 21 limits: $3,000 (1 dep) / $6,000 (2+ deps) — much lower than § 129 $5K
view.s129.s21.low_income: Low-income: § 21 credit better (35%); but DCAP saves FICA too
view.s129.s21.no_double_dip: NO double-dipping: § 21 credit-eligible expenses REDUCED by DCAP exclusion
view.s129.s21.rates: § 21 credit rate: 20-35% of expenses (sliding with AGI)
view.s129.s21.split_optimization: Split optimization: DCAP up to limit + § 21 credit on excess
view.s1291.h1.title: // § 1291 PFIC EXCESS DIST.
view.s1291.hint.intro: PFIC test: 75% passive income OR 50% passive assets (FMV avg). Default regime: 'excess distributions' taxed at HIGHEST ORDINARY RATE + INTEREST CHARGE back to start of holding period. Excess distribution = current dist > 125% of prior 3-yr avg. Gain on sale = entirely excess (no LTCG rate). Avoid via QEF election (§ 1295 = current inclusion) or MTM (§ 1296 = mark-to-market). Form 8621.
view.s1291.long_hold_note: Long PFIC holding period: interest charge compounds annually and can exceed the base tax for 10+ year holdings. Consider purging election (deemed sale at § 1291 rates) + QEF / MTM election going forward.
view.s1291.purge.allocate: Allocation: gain pro-rated DAY-BY-DAY across holding period
view.s1291.purge.current: Portion allocated to CURRENT year taxed at current top ordinary, no interest
view.s1291.purge.deemed: 'Deemed sale' purging election: recognize gain currently at § 1291 rates → then QEF / MTM clean
view.s1291.purge.late: Late QEF election: also requires purging via deemed sale
view.s1291.purge.pre_pfic: Pre-PFIC years not counted for interest charge (gain allocated to PFIC years only)
view.s1291.test.assets: Asset test: ≥ 50% of FMV avg assets produce passive income or held for production thereof
view.s1291.test.cfc_overlap: CFC overlap: CFC + PFIC overlap rules exclude CFC US shareholders from PFIC regime
view.s1291.test.either: EITHER test triggers PFIC status (not both required)
view.s1291.test.foreign_fund: Foreign mutual funds + ETFs almost always PFICs
view.s1291.test.income: Income test: ≥ 75% of gross income is passive (interest, div, royalties, rents, gains)
view.s1291.test.once: 'Once a PFIC, always a PFIC' — taint persists in shareholder's hands across years
view.s1291.test.startup: Start-up exception: first year of new corp may be exempt
view.s1295.ais.alternative: Alternative: shareholder may obtain via legal action if rights established
view.s1295.ais.consent: Must include consent to U.S. examination of corporate records
view.s1295.ais.contents: Contents: ordinary earnings + net capital gain attributable to shareholder + distributions + adjustments
view.s1295.ais.controlled: Controlled QEF: shareholder + family + related own > 50% — can compel AIS
view.s1295.ais.no_ais: No AIS = no QEF eligibility = default § 1291 regime applies
view.s1295.ais.protective: Protective Statement available if AIS not yet received — preserve QEF without AIS
view.s1295.ais.required: Required from foreign corp for QEF election to be valid
view.s1295.h1.title: // § 1295 PFIC QEF
view.s1295.hint.intro: QEF = annual current inclusion of pro-rata ORDINARY EARNINGS + NET CAPITAL GAIN of PFIC. Avoids § 1291 interest charge AND preserves CHARACTER (ordinary vs LTCG). Requires PFIC AIS (Annual Information Statement) from foreign corp. Election: Form 8621 with timely-filed return; first-year of shareholder ownership best. Late = combine with PURGING election.
view.s1295.mech.basis: Basis += inclusions; basis -= subsequent distributions of PTI
view.s1295.mech.character: Inclusion preserves character — ordinary earnings + LTCG separate inclusion lines
view.s1295.mech.deferral: Optional § 1294 deferral election: defer with interest charge until distribution
view.s1295.mech.eligibility: Must elect for first year shareholder treats foreign corp as PFIC
view.s1295.mech.late: Late election available only with PURGING (deemed sale at § 1291 rates)
view.s1295.mech.ptep: Previously taxed income (PTI) regime prevents double-taxation on later distributions
view.s1295.mech.timing: Inclusion in shareholder's year that ends with or within PFIC's taxable year
view.s1295.not_eligible_note: Not eligible for QEF without PFIC AIS + first-year (or purging) election. Default § 1291 regime applies: highest ordinary rate + interest charge back to start.
view.s1296.choice.holding_period: Length of holding: § 1291 interest charge accrues over holding period — bad for long holds
view.s1296.choice.purging: Purging distribution: can convert § 1291 PFIC to § 1295/§ 1296 (catch-up tax)
view.s1296.choice.s1295_problem: § 1295 requires PFIC to provide annual statement — many will NOT
view.s1296.choice.s1295_qef: § 1295 QEF: best if PFIC produces capital gain — preserves LTCG rate
view.s1296.choice.s1296_loss: § 1296 disadvantage: loss limited to unreversed inclusions
view.s1296.choice.s1296_only_marketable: § 1296 limited to marketable stock — many small/private PFICs don't qualify
view.s1296.choice.s1296_ordinary: § 1296 disadvantage: gain ordinary even on held > 1 yr
view.s1296.choice.s1296_simple: § 1296 MTM: simpler — no PFIC cooperation needed
view.s1296.f.required: Required ANNUALLY by US PFIC shareholder
view.s1296.f.s6038d_combine: Form 8938 + Form 8621 cross-references — different reporting regime
view.s1296.f.s6664_d_4: § 6664(d)(4) — civil fraud SOL never starts on PFIC items
view.s1296.f.thresholds: 2014+: Form 8621 required regardless of value (post-HIRE Act + Reg § 1.1298-1T)
view.s1296.h1.title: // § 1296 PFIC MARK-TO-MARKET ELECTION
view.s1296.hint.intro: Annual MTM election for marketable PFIC stock. Gain ordinary, loss limited to unreversed inclusions. Alternative to § 1291 punitive + § 1295 QEF.
view.s1296.mkt.adr_ok: ADR (American Depositary Receipt): often qualifying via US exchange
view.s1296.mkt.de_minimis: De minimis trading not qualifying — facts & circumstances
view.s1296.mkt.foreign: Foreign qualifying exchanges (LSE, Tokyo, Euronext, Toronto, ASX, Hong Kong, etc.)
view.s1296.mkt.regularly_traded: "Regularly traded" = 15+ days each quarter OR 1/6 of trading days
view.s1296.mkt.us: US national securities exchanges (NYSE, NASDAQ, NYSE American)
view.s1296.mtm.basis_adjusted: Basis ADJUSTED by gain/loss recognized — no double recognition
view.s1296.mtm.binding: BINDING for that year + all subsequent years (until stock sold or election revoked with IRS consent)
view.s1296.mtm.coordination_s1291: Coordinates with § 1291: prior years treated as § 1291 if not pedigreed QEF/MTM
view.s1296.mtm.election: Election made by attaching statement to timely-filed return for first year
view.s1296.mtm.fmv: Year-end FMV - adjusted basis = MTM gain or loss
view.s1296.mtm.form_8621: Form 8621 reports election + annual MTM gain/loss
view.s1296.mtm.gain_first_year: First-year election: catch-up — recognize built-in gain through purging
view.s1296.mtm.gain_ordinary: GAIN: ORDINARY income (no LTCG even if held > 1 year)
view.s1296.mtm.loss_limited: LOSS: ORDINARY deduction limited to "unreversed inclusions" (cumulative prior gains minus prior losses)
view.s1296.pfic.asset: Asset test: 50%+ of assets (FMV or basis) produce passive income
view.s1296.pfic.income: Income test: 75%+ of gross income is PASSIVE
view.s1296.pfic.passive_examples: Passive: interest, dividends, royalties, rents, annuities
view.s1296.pfic.s1297_b_2: § 1297(b)(2) — exclude active rents + dividends from operating subsidiary
view.s1296.tbl.s1291_treat: Throwback over holding period + interest charge + highest rate
view.s1296.tbl.s1296_treat: Annual FMV mark; gain ordinary + loss limited to unreversed
view.s1297.cfc_note: § 1298(b)(8) CFC OVERLAP exception: this US 10% shareholder of a CFC is NOT subject to PFIC regime. Subpart F + GILTI apply instead. Critical when foreign sub has dual character — must verify CFC status (50%+ US ownership). Form 5471 filing replaces / supplements Form 8621 PFIC reporting.
view.s1297.cons.attribution: Pass-through entities: PFIC status flows to ultimate US owner
view.s1297.cons.cross_border_planning: Cross-border M&A: pre-deal PFIC analysis critical
view.s1297.cons.default: § 1291 default: top ordinary rate + interest charge on excess distributions / sale gain
view.s1297.cons.estate: Estate planning: gift / inheritance triggers § 1291 unless purged
view.s1297.cons.mtm: § 1296 MTM: mark-to-market on tradable PFIC stock
view.s1297.cons.qef: § 1295 QEF election: annual current inclusion + preserve character
view.s1297.cons.taint: 'Once PFIC, always PFIC' until purged via deemed sale
view.s1297.exc.5pct_passive: 5% de minimis: ≤ 5% income test exception in cleansing years
view.s1297.exc.banking: § 1297(b)(2)(A) active banking: ≥ 4 financial professionals + customary loans + deposits
view.s1297.exc.cfc_overlap: § 1298(b)(8) CFC overlap: CFC US 10% shareholder NOT PFIC for that shareholder
view.s1297.exc.commodities: Active commodities dealer: ≥ 75% income from regular trade dealer activity
view.s1297.exc.foreign_personal: Foreign personal services: NOT passive if performed by service-providers
view.s1297.exc.related_party: Related-party rent / royalty: NOT passive (look-through to active business)
view.s1297.exc.startup: § 1298(b)(2) STARTUP: first year of new active business may escape PFIC if not PFIC in first 3 yrs
view.s1297.h1.title: // § 1297 PFIC DEFINITION
view.s1297.hint.intro: PFIC if EITHER: (1) 75%+ income passive OR (2) 50%+ assets produce passive income (or held for such). 'Once a PFIC, always a PFIC' in shareholder's hands until purged. Look-through rule: ≥ 25%-owned subsidiaries taxed on look-through basis. Startup exception § 1298(b)(2): first year of new corp may be exempt.
